trades.market: Will the rate of CPI inflation be above 3.4% for the year ending in March 2026?
trades.catalyst: March CPI data release on April 10th, 2026. Expectations of higher energy prices impacting CPI.
trades.risk_notes: Unexpected drop in energy prices or revisions to CPI calculation methodology.

trades.market: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
trades.catalyst: March CPI data release on April 10th, 2026. Expectations of lower core inflation than headline.
trades.risk_notes: Unexpected surge in core inflation components.

trades.market: Will the Federal Reserve cut rates before 2027?
trades.catalyst: Hawkish Fed stance and potential for no rate cuts in 2026.
trades.risk_notes: Dovish shift in Fed policy due to economic slowdown or geopolitical events.

trades.market: Will the high temp in NYC be <53° on Apr 9, 2026?
trades.catalyst: Weather forecasts indicate a high temperature above 53°F in NYC on April 9, 2026.
trades.risk_notes: Unexpected cold front or weather anomaly.

trades.market: Will the high temp in Chicago be >72° on Apr 9, 2026?
trades.catalyst: Weather forecasts indicate a high temperature below 72°F in Chicago on April 9, 2026.
trades.risk_notes: Unexpected warm front or weather anomaly.

trades.market: Will the high temp in Miami be 78-79° on Apr 9, 2026?
trades.catalyst: Weather forecasts indicate a high temperature outside the 78-79°F range in Miami on April 9, 2026.

avoid.reason: Low volume and high volatility in crypto markets.

notes: The macro environment is currently inflationary due to rising energy prices and persistent tariff effects. The Fed is expected to maintain a hawkish stance, limiting the possibility of rate cuts in the near term. Upcoming CPI data release will be a key catalyst for market movements.
